summary: A brief, humorous post written the day after the 2004 U.S. presidential election offering a practical tip: WD-40 or kerosene can remove bumper stickers. The implicit joke is that post-election, people on the losing side will want to scrape off their political bumper stickers. The entire post is just two sentences, making it one of the most concise political commentaries possible.
key_points: WD-40 (mostly kerosene) effectively removes bumper stickers
The timing—one day after the 2004 election—is the entire joke
Practical maker advice doubles as dry political humor
money_quotes: WD-40 (or straight kerosene, which is what WD-40 is mostly composed of) takes off bumper stickers.
It's handy stuff to have around the shop–you'll thank me for it.
key_insight: Sometimes the best political commentary is a practical tip delivered with perfect timing.
